name: Lackland AFB
profile: Military-friendly Lackland AFB housing snapshot anchored to the San Antonio metro, built for PCSUnited base-level market overviews.

mortgage_assumptions.defaults_note: Based on March 2026 San Antonio VA loan rates.

notes: Texas property taxes materially affect true monthly ownership cost.
Insurance and HOA should be included in all-in housing cost comparisons.

avg_home_mortgage_monthly.notes: VA loan estimate for San Antonio area

crime_status.installation: Extremely safe; gated military access with 24/7 security.
crime_status.metro_san_antonio: Property crime rates are moderate; focus on West Side safety awareness.

school_quality.off_base: Varies; Northside ISD is generally preferred for off-base residents.

rental_vacancy.notes: Healthy inventory for military renters in 2026.

rental_metrics.notes: Based on 3-bedroom average rent and healthy military-renter inventory.

military_lifestyle_fit.description: Gateway to the Air Force; massive military infrastructure and local support.

financial_brief.affordability_summary: San Antonio remains one of the most affordable major military metros.
financial_brief.buyer_opportunity: Strong equity potential in West/Southwest expansion areas.

market_bluf.bluf_summary: Lackland area offers some of the best military home-buying value in the US.

summary_points: Lackland AFB sits inside one of the more affordable major Texas metros for military buyers entering the market.
Current San Antonio market posture is buyer-leaning, with increasing inventory and relatively healthy leverage.
The Lackland-linked submarket shows especially strong military-fit and entry-level value.
Property taxes and insurance still materially shape true monthly ownership cost in this market.
West and Southwest growth corridors appear to offer stronger buyer opportunity than more expensive Texas metros.
opportunities: Buyer leverage appears stronger here than in many larger Texas metros.
Entry-level and mid-range inventory remains comparatively accessible near the Lackland corridor.
Military-connected demand supports long-term housing relevance around Lackland AFB.
West and Southwest expansion areas may offer stronger upside for budget-conscious military buyers.
risks: Texas property taxes can erode affordability if buyers only focus on purchase price.
Insurance and utilities can widen the gap between principal-and-interest and true all-in payment.
Neighborhood quality and school quality vary materially across the metro.
Property crime exposure is more moderate off-installation, especially on the West Side.
buyer_notes: Lackland AFB is attractive for buyers who want a lower entry price than Austin or Dallas while staying inside a major Texas metro.
The real decision should be made on all-in monthly ownership cost, not just mortgage principal and interest.
School quality and neighborhood-level variation matter enough that citywide averages should not be the final filter.
This market is stronger for disciplined buyers who compare taxes, insurance, commute, and future resale flexibility together.
seller_notes: Sellers should price realistically against rising inventory rather than assume aggressive premium pricing.
Move-in-ready presentation and sharper pricing discipline will matter more in a buyer-leaning setup.
Homes near stable Lackland military demand nodes may still attract resilient buyer pools.
investor_angles: Healthy renter inventory and military turnover support rental relevance, but investors should stress-test taxes, insurance, and vacancy.
The Lackland corridor can work best for investors focused on durable occupancy rather than purely speculative appreciation.
Bedroom-specific rent bands make this market more useful for practical underwriting than broad metro averages alone.
buyer_guidance: Use all-in payment, not just list price, as the primary screen.
Compare taxes, insurance, commute, and school quality before locking a neighborhood.
This market rewards disciplined buyers more than emotional stretch buyers.
A slightly lower purchase price can materially improve monthly flexibility in Texas.
seller_guidance: Price against current inventory and days on market, not older peak assumptions.
Condition and presentation matter more when buyers have leverage.
Military-adjacent demand can be a strength if framed correctly in listing positioning.
landlord_notes: Military renter demand can improve occupancy durability near the Lackland area.
Investors should still model vacancy, tax drag, insurance, and maintenance conservatively.
A good rental here is about stable spread, not just headline affordability.

base_profile.market_label: San Antonio / JBSA-Lackland Military Housing Market
base_profile.installation_type: Training / Medical / Cyber / Joint Base Support
base_profile.host_or_major_command: Joint Base San Antonio / 502nd Air Base Wing
base_profile.primary_mission_summary: JBSA-Lackland is a major Air Force training and support installation on the west side of San Antonio. The base is strongly associated with Basic Military Training, technical training, medical support, cyber/intelligence-related missions, and joint-base support functions.
base_profile.pcs_personality: Training-heavy, high-traffic, newcomer-heavy, family-relevant, and highly tied to gate access, BAH fit, commute timing, and neighborhood selection.
base_profile.base_bluf: Lackland is not just a San Antonio housing decision. It is a gate-access, commute, BAH, school-zone, and neighborhood-fit decision. Families should compare nearby areas by realistic gate commute and monthly housing pressure instead of judging only by distance to the base.

user_positioning.military_family_bluf: Prioritize gate access, school-zone verification, BAH fit, and commute testing before signing a lease or purchase contract.
user_positioning.single_airman_bluf: Prioritize commute simplicity, affordability, roommate/rental flexibility, and access to food, gyms, and daily services.
user_positioning.realtor_bluf: The strongest Lackland housing conversations are about gate access, BAH realism, commute risk, and family lifestyle fit — not generic San Antonio market stats.
user_positioning.investor_bluf: Rental demand near Lackland is influenced by training volume, PCS movement, BMT graduations, medical/cyber presence, and military-family turnover.

base_map_image.alt: PCSUnited public-facing JBSA-Lackland base orientation map showing gates, family housing, major services, and nearby recommended neighborhoods.
base_map_image.title: JBSA-Lackland Base & Housing Overview
base_map_image.caption: Public-facing PCS orientation map for gates, housing, major services, and nearby neighborhood strategy.
base_map_image.safety_note: This is not an official DoD map. It is a public-facing PCS orientation graphic using publicly available information and should not be used for access control, emergency routing, or official navigation.

visitor_control_center.name: Luke East Visitor Control Center
best_for: Family members without routine installation access
visitor_control_center.note: Use official JBSA visitor information before arrival. Access requirements, sponsor rules, gate procedures, and hours can change.

gates.name: Luke East Gate
gates.commute_notes: Strong default gate for visitors and newcomer routing. Good anchor point for PCS orientation.
gates.user_warning: Verify current gate status with official JBSA sources before travel.

gates.name: Valley Hi Gate
best_for: Potranco / Far West San Antonio access depending on route
gates.commute_notes: Useful for users living west or southwest of the installation.
gates.user_warning: Commute can vary heavily by school traffic, work schedule, and peak gate flow.

gates.name: McMullen Gate
gates.commute_notes: Important alternate 24/7 gate for commute planning.
gates.user_warning: Verify route suitability based on the user's neighborhood and duty location.

gates.name: Security Hill Gate
gates.commute_notes: Important for certain duty locations, but not a universal default gate.
gates.user_warning: Limited hours can affect commute reliability. Do not assume this gate is available outside posted windows.

gates.name: Selfridge East Gate
gates.commute_notes: Limited morning-only access makes this a specialized commute gate.
gates.user_warning: Not recommended as a primary planning gate unless the user's schedule matches posted hours.

gates.name: Selfridge West Gate
gates.hours: Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, Friday: 5:00 a.m. to 8:00 a.m. and 3:00 p.m. to 5:00 p.m. outbound only; Wednesday: 5:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. inbound and outbound
gates.commute_notes: Useful only when the user's schedule aligns with posted access rules.
gates.user_warning: Outbound-only periods matter. Do not use this as a default route without checking official hours.

gates.name: Growdon / Commercial Gate
gates.commute_notes: Useful for commercial or delivery planning, not a default family commute gate.
gates.user_warning: Movers and commercial deliveries should verify procedures through official base access guidance.

gates.name: Luke West Gate
gates.hours: Closed per current JBSA gate listing
gates.commute_notes: Do not use as a default route while listed as closed.
gates.user_warning: Always verify official JBSA gate information before travel.

major_services.name: Wilford Hall Ambulatory Surgical Center
major_services.hours: Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, Friday 7:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m.; Wednesday 9:15 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. per public medical page
major_services.user_note: Treat this as a major medical facility for PCS planning. Verify emergency care options, clinic availability, and pharmacy details directly with official medical sources.

major_services.name: Lackland Commissary
major_services.user_note: Commissary access can materially affect monthly food budget for eligible users.

major_services.name: JBSA Lackland Main Exchange
major_services.address: 2180 Reese Street, Bldg. 1385, Lackland AFB, TX 78236
major_services.hours: Mon-Sat 0900-1900 per Exchange store locator; verify current hours before travel
major_services.user_note: Important daily-life anchor for new arrivals and families.

major_services.name: Military Clothing Store
major_services.address: 1461 Patrick Street, Bldg. 7037, Lackland AFB, TX 78236
major_services.hours: Mon-Fri 0800-1700 per Exchange store locator; verify current hours
major_services.user_note: Useful for military-specific shopping needs after arrival.

major_services.name: JBSA-Lackland Military Housing Office
major_services.hours: Monday-Friday, 7:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m.; closed holidays
major_services.user_note: Military families should contact the housing office before committing to on-base or off-base housing.

major_services.name: Unaccompanied Housing Office
major_services.hours: Monday-Friday, 7:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m.; closed holidays
major_services.user_note: Eligibility and availability should be verified directly with the housing office.

major_services.name: Bank / Credit Union / ATM Services
major_services.address: Verify current branch or ATM location before travel
major_services.user_note: Specific bank or credit union locations may change. Use current official branch locator or base directory before routing users.

major_services.name: Legal / Finance / Admin Services
major_services.address: Verify current office location before travel
major_services.user_note: Use this as a planning category in the Base tab; exact office routing should point to official base directory links.

major_services.name: Child / Youth / Family Support Services
major_services.address: Verify current CDC, youth center, and school liaison locations before travel
major_services.user_note: Critical for families. Exact program availability, waitlists, and eligibility should be verified with official JBSA resources.

on_base_housing.housing_type: Privatized and military housing support environment
on_base_housing.housing_office_hours: Monday-Friday, 7:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m.; closed holidays
on_base_housing.map_zone: central / Fairchild Street and family housing support area
best_for: Families wanting to reduce commute uncertainty
watchouts: Availability and waitlists can change
On-base housing may capture BAH differently than off-base renting
Pet policies and breed restrictions should be verified
School assignment must be verified by exact address
Home age, condition, and neighborhood fit should be reviewed before commitment
on_base_housing.pcsu_strategy_note: Compare on-base housing against off-base rent and mortgage options. The best choice depends on rank, dependents, BAH, commute tolerance, school priorities, pet needs, and how long the assignment may last.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Westover Hills
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Strong family and renter fit with newer housing, retail, medical access, and reasonable Lackland access.
best_for: Users wanting retail and services nearby
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 10-20 minutes depending on gate, time of day, and exact address
watchouts: Traffic can build around Loop 410, Highway 151, and nearby retail corridors
Verify exact commute during expected duty hours
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Good first-look area for families who want convenience without being too far from Lackland.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Potranco / Far West San Antonio
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Growing west-side housing corridor with newer subdivisions and many military-family housing options.
best_for: BAH-conscious buyers comparing price and space
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 20-35 minutes depending on exact neighborhood, gate, and traffic
likely_gate_logic: Luke East Gate depending on route
watchouts: Fast-growing area can create congestion
Commute may look easy on a map but feel different during peak hours
School zones vary by exact address
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Strong family and VA-buyer candidate if the user accepts a longer commute for newer housing and more space.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Alamo Ranch
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Popular northwest suburban area with strong family appeal, larger-home options, retail, and master-planned community feel.
best_for: Users prioritizing lifestyle and schools over shortest commute
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 25-40 minutes depending on gate, school traffic, and Loop 1604 conditions
likely_gate_logic: McMullen Gate depending on final route
watchouts: Loop 1604 and Culebra-area traffic can be frustrating
Distance may feel larger during early report times
Not ideal for users demanding the shortest commute
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Good for families who want a stronger suburban lifestyle and can tolerate commute variability.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Valley Hi / Marbach Area
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Very close to Lackland and often budget-relevant, but requires careful property-level review.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 5-15 minutes depending on gate and exact address
likely_gate_logic: Luke East Gate depending on location
watchouts: Property condition can vary widely
Users should review street-level safety, schools, and home condition carefully
Do not choose purely because it is close
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Best treated as a commute-first and budget-first zone that needs stronger screening.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Helotes
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Quieter northwest suburban option with lifestyle appeal, but farther from Lackland.
best_for: Buyers prioritizing quality of life over commute
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 30-45+ minutes depending on gate, traffic, and exact address
likely_gate_logic: Luke East Gate depending on route
watchouts: Longer commute may wear on early-report personnel
Budget may stretch depending on home size and market conditions
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Lifestyle-first option, not commute-first. Best for families who value the area enough to accept the drive.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Castroville / Medina Valley
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: More space and newer-build potential west of San Antonio, with longer commute tradeoffs.
best_for: Users comfortable living farther from central San Antonio
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 25-45+ minutes depending on exact address, gate, and traffic
watchouts: Longer drives for base services and San Antonio amenities
Verify commute during expected duty hours
School zones and services vary by exact address
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: A space-and-lifestyle play for families willing to trade convenience for room and quieter surroundings.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Downtown / Southtown
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Urban lifestyle option with entertainment and culture access, but less family/commute optimized for Lackland.
best_for: Users prioritizing nightlife and city access
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 20-35 minutes depending on route, traffic, and gate
watchouts: Parking, rent, and commute may reduce value
Less ideal for families prioritizing schools and space
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Better for lifestyle-focused singles or couples than family-first PCS planning.

commute_intelligence.commute_bluf: For Lackland, gate access matters more than straight-line distance. A home that looks close may not be the best choice if the gate, road pattern, or duty schedule creates friction.
primary_commute_factors: Exact duty location on or near the installation
Shift schedule or early report time

commute_bands.label: Close-in
commute_bands.fit: Best for users prioritizing proximity and daily convenience
commute_bands.watchout: Close-in areas still need property, school, and safety screening.

commute_bands.label: Balanced
commute_bands.fit: Often the best balance between housing quality, services, and commute
commute_bands.watchout: Traffic and gate choice can still change the real commute.

commute_bands.label: Lifestyle tradeoff
commute_bands.fit: May work for families wanting larger homes, quieter areas, or specific schools
commute_bands.watchout: Can become painful for early reporting, shift work, or frequent base errands.

gate_selection_logic: Start with the user's likely duty location.
Check limited-hour gates only as optional commute helpers.
Compare the commute during the user's actual report window.
Do not choose a neighborhood based only on mileage.
commute_intelligence.user_instruction: Before signing a lease or contract, users should test the commute from the exact property address to the expected gate during the same time window they would normally report to work.

bah_market_reality.bah_bluf: Lackland-area housing decisions should compare BAH against rent, mortgage, utilities, debt, and commute. BAH may support renting more comfortably than buying for some ranks unless debt is low, down payment is available, or the user targets a lower price band.
recommended_calculations: BAH with dependents vs estimated rent
BAH without dependents vs estimated rent
Total housing cost vs 30% income lane
Residual monthly income after expenses and housing
bah_market_reality.base_tab_message: Show BAH as a decision filter, not a promise of affordability. The Base tab should push users into the PCSUnited financial calculator when the housing gap looks tight.

family_readiness.family_bluf: Families PCSing to Lackland should verify school zones, childcare options, medical access, commute reality, and housing availability before choosing a neighborhood.

school_guidance.school_zone_warning: Always verify school assignment by exact address before signing a lease or purchase contract.
school_guidance.nearby_school_district_note: Common Lackland-area school district research may include Northside ISD, Medina Valley ISD, Southwest ISD, and other address-specific districts depending on the neighborhood.
school_guidance.school_liaison_note: Use official school liaison resources where available for military school transition support.

childcare_guidance.cdc_note: Child Development Center availability, waitlists, and eligibility should be verified directly with official base resources.
childcare_guidance.youth_program_note: Youth programs and school-age care can materially affect family housing decisions.

medical_guidance.medical_warning: Verify clinic availability, pharmacy procedures, and emergency care options directly with official medical sources before relying on any single facility.

family_readiness.spouse_employment_note: San Antonio can offer broader spouse employment options than smaller duty stations, but commute, childcare, and credentialing should be considered together.

before_arrival: Verify official gate and Visitor Control Center hours.
Confirm sponsor contact and unit reporting instructions.
Confirm lodging or temporary housing plan.
Review housing office guidance before signing any lease or purchase contract.
Compare BAH against rent, mortgage, utilities, and debt.
Shortlist neighborhoods by likely gate and commute band.
Verify school zones by exact property address.
Check pet policies for on-base housing, rentals, and temporary lodging.
first_week: Visit or contact the housing office if still deciding between on-base and off-base options.
Drive the commute from target neighborhoods during expected report time.
Tour properties before committing when possible.
Avoid choosing purely by distance or rent price.
first_30_days: Finalize housing only after comparing commute, BAH fit, school zones, and total monthly cost.
Save emergency, medical, gate, and housing office contact information.
Update PCSUnited profile and financial readiness dashboard with final housing numbers.
Review whether the selected area supports the user's assignment length and family needs.

pcs_watchouts.title: Gate hours can change
pcs_watchouts.message: Use official JBSA gate pages before travel or commute planning.

pcs_watchouts.title: Distance is not the same as commute
pcs_watchouts.message: Neighborhoods should be compared by realistic gate access and traffic timing.

pcs_watchouts.title: School zones are address-specific
pcs_watchouts.message: Never assume a school assignment from neighborhood name alone.

pcs_watchouts.title: BAH is not the full affordability picture
pcs_watchouts.message: Users must compare rent or mortgage against income, debt, expenses, utilities, and savings goals.

pcs_watchouts.title: Close-in housing varies widely
pcs_watchouts.message: Some areas near base may be very convenient but require stronger property-level review.

pcs_watchouts.title: Limited-hour gates can distort commute estimates
pcs_watchouts.message: A route that works at 7 a.m. may not work at 7 p.m. if the gate schedule changes.

pcs_watchouts.title: On-base housing is not automatically the best financial answer
pcs_watchouts.message: Compare waitlist, BAH capture, commute, home condition, schools, and lifestyle before deciding.

likely_questions: Can I afford to buy near Lackland?
buyer_personas.agent_strategy: Lead with BAH realism, monthly payment clarity, commute testing, and VA loan education.

likely_questions: Where do military families usually live?
Which school zones should I research?
buyer_personas.agent_strategy: Compare Westover Hills, Potranco/Far West, Alamo Ranch, and on-base housing against commute and monthly cost.

buyer_personas.persona: Single airman / technical training / short-term renter
likely_questions: Where should I avoid signing too fast?
buyer_personas.agent_strategy: Keep recommendations practical: commute, rent, safety, property condition, and lease flexibility.

likely_questions: Should I buy if I may be here longer?
Where can I balance family and commute?
buyer_personas.agent_strategy: Use assignment length, family needs, and rent-vs-buy math to guide the recommendation.

listing_angles: Convenient to Luke East Visitor Control Center
Easy access to Loop 410, Highway 90, and Highway 151
common_objections: Affordability gap between BAH and mortgage
recommended_agent_behavior: Always recommend school-zone verification by address.
Use payment math, not just listing price.
Compare on-base housing as a legitimate alternative.
Avoid generic San Antonio advice when the user is assigned to Lackland.

source_notes.label: Air Force Housing - Joint Base San Antonio

base_profile.disclaimer: PCSUnited is not an official DoD source. This Base tab is intended for public-facing PCS orientation, housing research, and relocation planning. Users should verify gate hours, access requirements, medical services, housing availability, school zones, and official procedures directly with the installation or official service provider before travel, lease signing, or home purchase.

demographics.provider: US Census Bureau / World Population Review

parser_notes: This Lackland AFB file now includes the TOR Cities Overview parser fields: slug, name, state_code, profile, market_metrics, metrics, ownership_costs, costs, rental_metrics, summary_points, risks, opportunities, buyer_notes, seller_notes, investor_angles, and guidance arrays.
Base-facing label is set to Lackland AFB, while San Antonio is preserved as the surrounding metro/submarket context.
property_tax_rate and insurance_rate are normalized to decimal form for deterministic math, with percent aliases preserved for display compatibility.

This file now includes base_profile for the PCSUnited Base tab, including gate orientation, visitor control center, major services, on-base housing, recommended nearby neighborhoods, commute intelligence, BAH market guidance, PCS watchouts, and source notes.
